scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT, BENGALURU

ORIGINAL SUIT NO. _____ OF 2024

BETWEEN:

ANITA SHARMA, D/o {{FATHER_NAME}}, AGED ABOUT 52 YEARS,
R/O INDIRANAGAR, BENGALURU, KARNATAKA – {{PIN_CODE}}. ... PLAINTIFF

AND:

RAJESH REDDY, S/o {{FATHER_NAME}}, AGED ABOUT 60 YEARS,
R/O HSR LAYOUT, BENGALURU, KARNATAKA – {{PIN_CODE}}. ... DEFENDANT

PLAINT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT

1. The Plaintiff is the absolute owner of the funds and is seeking specific performance of the Agreement to Sell described herein. The Defendant is the owner of the schedule property and is bound to sell the same to the Plaintiff.

2. The subject matter of the suit is the residential property bearing Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, more particularly described in the Schedule of Property annexed hereto.

3. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into an Agreement to Sell in respect of the schedule property for a total consideration of Rs.85,00,000/-.

4. As per the terms of the Agreement dated 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs.25,00,000/- to the Defendant on the date of the agreement.

5. The advance payment of Rs.25,00,000/- was made via RTGS bearing {{UTR_NUMBER}} on 10.01.2024.

6. The balance consideration of Rs.60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024 against execution of the sale deed.

7. READINESS AND WILLINGNESS
The Plaintiff has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the contract. The Plaintiff has kept the balance consideration of Rs.60,00,000/- ready and available for payment to the Defendant against execution of the sale deed as per the agreement terms.

8. On 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff refusing to execute the sale deed. The Defendant claimed in the letter that he had received a higher offer for the schedule property.

9. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the sale deed in terms of the Agreement to Sell.

10. The Defendant refused to comply with the legal notice and failed to execute the sale deed despite the Plaintiff's readiness and willingness to perform.

11. CAUSE OF ACTION
The cause of action arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant refused to execute the sale deed. The cause of action continues to subsist as the Defendant has not performed his obligations.

12. JURISDICTION
The schedule property is situated within the territorial limits of Bengaluru. This Court has jurisdiction to try this suit under Section 9 and Section 16 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

13. COURT FEE
The appropriate court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is paid on this plaint under the Karnataka Court-Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

14. LIMITATION
The suit is filed within three years from the date of refusal as per Article 54 of the Limitation Act, 1963. The period of limitation is three years from the date fixed for performance or when the plaintiff has notice that performance is refused.

15. INTEREST
The Plaintiff is entitled to interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. The Plaintiff claims interest on the advance amount at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from the date of breach until realization.

16. LEGAL GROUNDS
The Plaintiff relies on Section 10 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, which mandates specific performance of contracts relating to immovable property. The Plaintiff relies on Section 16 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, regarding personal bars to relief and readiness and willingness. The Plaintiff relies on Section 10 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, regarding valid contracts. The Plaintiff relies on Section 53A of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, regarding part performance.

17. PRAYER
The Plaintiff prays for the following reliefs:
(a) Decree for specific performance of the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024 directing the Defendant to execute the sale deed in favour of the Plaintiff;
(b) Direction to the Defendant to deliver possession of the schedule property to the Plaintiff under Section 22 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963;
(c) Payment of interest on the advance amount at {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from 05.06.2024 until realization;
(d) Costs of the suit;
(e) Pendente lite and future interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
(f) Such other relief as this Court deems fit to grant in the interest of justice.

18. LIST OF DOCUMENTS
The Plaintiff relies on the following documents:
ANNEXURE-A: Original Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024.
ANNEXURE-B: RTGS Receipt (UTR: {{UTR_NUMBER}}).
ANNEXURE-C: Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024.
ANNEXURE-D: Legal Notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt.

SCHEDULE OF PROPERTY

Residential Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft.
Survey No.: {{SURVEY_NUMBER}}
East Boundary: {{EAST_BOUNDARY}}
West Boundary: {{WEST_BOUNDARY}}
North Boundary: {{NORTH_BOUNDARY}}
South Boundary: {{SOUTH_BOUNDARY}}

VERIFICATION

I, ANITA SHARMA, the Plaintiff herein, do hereby declare that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 10 are true to my knowledge and the contents of paragraphs 11 to 17 are based on legal advice which I believe to be true. Verified at Bengaluru on this {{DAY}} day of {{MONTH}}, 2024.

PLAINTIFF

ADVOCATE BLOCK

{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Enrollment No.: {{ENROLLMENT_NUMBER}}
Address: {{OFFICE_ADDRESS}}, BENGALURU
Mobile: {{MOBILE_NUMBER}}

COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE PRINCIPAL JUDGE, COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION) AT BENGALURU

COMMERCIAL SUIT NO. _____ OF 2024

BETWEEN:

TECHSOFT SOLUTIONS PVT LTD, A COMPANY REGISTERED UNDER THE COMPANIES ACT, HAVING ITS REGISTERED OFFICE AT MG ROAD, BENGALURU, KARNATAKA – {{PIN}}, REPRESENTED BY ITS DIRECTOR PRIYA MENON, AGED ABOUT 40 YEARS. ... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

CLOUDBRIDGE SYSTEMS LLP, A LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIP HAVING ITS OFFICE AT WHITEFIELD, BENGALURU, KARNATAKA – {{PIN}}, REPRESENTED BY ITS DESIGNATED PARTNER VIKRAM SINGH, AGED ABOUT 48 YEARS. ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND REFUND OF ADVANCE PAYMENT

THE PLAINTIFF MOST RESPECTFULLY SUBMITS AS FOLLOWS:

1. The Plaintiff is a company registered under the Companies Act, having its registered office at MG Road, Bengaluru, Karnataka. The Plaintiff is represented by its Director, Priya Menon, aged about 40 years, who is duly authorized to file this suit. The Plaintiff is engaged in the business of software solutions and technology services.

2. The Defendant is a Limited Liability Partnership having its office at Whitefield, Bengaluru, Karnataka. The Defendant is represented by its Designated Partner, Vikram Singh, aged about 48 years. The Defendant is engaged in the business of software development and related services.

3. This Court has jurisdiction to try this suit under Section 9 and Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the cause of action arose within the limits of Bengaluru and the Defendant resides within the jurisdiction. This suit is filed before the Commercial Court as the dispute is a commercial dispute under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and the specified value exceeds {{AMOUNT}}.

4. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a Software Development Agreement with the Defendant for the development of an ERP system. The total contract value agreed upon was Rs. 45,00,000/-. The terms of the agreement obligated the Defendant to develop and deliver the software modules as per the agreed timeline.

5. Pursuant to the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid the first milestone payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Defendant on 01.02.2024. The payment was made via NEFT bearing UTR Number ICIC20240201456789. The Plaintiff has fulfilled its financial obligation under the contract for the said milestone.

6. As per the terms of the Software Development Agreement, the Defendant was obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system by 01.05.2024. Time was the essence of the contract regarding the delivery of the software modules.

7. The Defendant failed to deliver any module by the deadline of 01.05.2024. The Defendant did not provide the agreed-upon software deliverables despite the receipt of the advance payment.

8. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project without completing the development work. The Defendant ceased communication and performance under the Agreement, thereby committing a breach of contract.

9. Due to the non-delivery and abandonment of the project by the Defendant, the Plaintiff suffered additional loss of Rs. 12,00,000/- due to delay in operations caused by non-delivery. This loss has been assessed and documented by the Plaintiff's auditor.

10. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the Defendant calling upon the Defendant to refund the advance payment and compensate for the losses suffered. The Defendant has failed to comply with the notice or return the advance amount.

11. The cause of action arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant abandoned the project and breached the contract. The breach constitutes a single event occurring on the date of abandonment. The subsequent legal notice was issued to mitigate damages and comply with procedural requirements.

12. The suit is within limitation as per Article 55 of the Limitation Act.

13. The breach occurred on 15.06.2024, and the suit is filed within three years from the date of breach.

14. The Plaintiff has paid the requisite court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} on this plaint as per the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, applicable to the Commercial Court.

15. The Plaintiff states regarding Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, that the Plaintiff has issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024. The Plaintiff is ready and willing to undergo pre-institution mediation as per the provisions of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, if required by this Court.

16. The Plaintiff relies on Sections 37, 39, 55, 73, and 74 of the Indian Contract Act.

17. Section 37 mandates performance of contractual promises. Section 39 deals with the effect of refusal to perform. Section 55 establishes the effect of failure to perform at a fixed time. Section 73 provides for compensation for loss caused by breach. Section 74 provides for compensation for breach of contract where a sum is named or penalty is stipulated. The Plaintiff also relies on Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, for the grant of interest.

18. The Plaintiff prays for judgment and decree against the Defendant as follows: (a) Decreeing the suit for recovery of Rs. 32,00,000/- being Rs. 20,00,000/- as refund of advance payment and Rs. 12,00,000/- as consequential damages;
(b) Awarding interest at 18% per annum on Rs. 20,00,000/- from 01.02.2024 till realization, covering pre-suit, pendente lite, and future interest;
(c) Awarding interest at 18% per annum on Rs. 12,00,000/- from 15.06.2024 till realization;
(d) Awarding costs of the suit;
(e) Granting such other relief as this Court deems fit;
(f) Passing any further orders as deemed necessary in the interest of justice.

19. The Plaintiff lists the following documents relied upon in support of the suit:
Annexure A: Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024;
Annexure B: NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789);
Annexure C: Email correspondence showing abandonment;
Annexure D: Loss assessment report by auditor;
Annexure E: Legal notice dated 01.07.2024.

20. VERIFICATION: I, Priya Menon, Director of TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd, do hereby verify that the contents of Paragraphs 1 to 17 of the plaint are true and correct to my knowledge and belief. Verified at Bengaluru on this __ day of __ 2024.

21. STATEMENT OF TRUTH (ORDER VI RULE 15A): The Plaintiff verifies that the contents of this plaint are true and correct. No part of it is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom. This statement is made in compliance with Order VI Rule 15A of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, applicable to Commercial Disputes.

PLACE: BENGALURU

DATE: __.__.2024

{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
{{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
{{ADDRESS}}
{{MOBILE}}

ADVOCATE FOR PLAINTIFF
